# Appendix {#sec-appendix}

## A.1 Source Code Repositories

The complete source code for the UpHeal system is distributed across the following GitHub repositories:

| Repository | Description | URL |
|-----------|-------------|-----|
| UpHeal-Doc | Thesis documentation, Quarto project, and all writing | <https://github.com/Upheal1/UpHeal-Doc> |
| UpHeal-Backend | Python FastAPI backend, microservices, AI pipeline | <https://github.com/Upheal1/UpHeal-Backend> |
| UpHeal-Frontend | Flutter mobile application | <https://github.com/Upheal1/UpHeal-Frontend> |
| UpHeal-Admin | Therapist dashboard (planned) | <https://github.com/Upheal1/UpHeal-Admin> |

All repositories are licensed under the MIT License.

## A.2 Project Structure

The thesis project follows this directory layout:

```
UpHeal-Doc/
├── _quarto.yml              # Quarto book configuration
├── index.qmd                 # Cover page
├── acknowledgements.qmd      # Acknowledgements
├── references.bib            # IEEE-formatted bibliography
├── chapters/
│   ├── 01-introduction.qmd
│   ├── 02-background.qmd
│   ├── 03-methodology.qmd
│   ├── 04-results-discussions.qmd
│   ├── 05-conclusions.qmd
│   └── appendix.qmd
├── diagrams/                 # External diagrams (draw.io, etc.)
├── images/                   # Generated chart images
├── scripts/
│   └── generate_charts.py    # Chart generation script
├── results/                  # CSV result files for charts
├── styles/
│   └── ieee.csl              # IEEE citation style
├── output/                   # Rendered PDF/DOCX output
└── docs/                     # Team documentation
    ├── PLAN.md
    ├── PROGRESS.md
    ├── SYNC_PLAN.md
    ├── BRANCH_STRATEGY.md
    ├── STYLE_GUIDE.md
    ├── TEAM_ASSIGNMENTS.md
    ├── SPRINT_48H.md
    └── CHANGELOG.md
```

## A.3 Reproducing Charts

The charts in @sec-results-discussions are generated from the `scripts/generate_charts.py` script. To reproduce:

```bash
# Install dependencies
pip install matplotlib numpy pandas

# Generate charts
python scripts/generate_charts.py

# Rebuild the thesis
quarto render
```

The script supports reading from CSV files in the `results/` directory. If CSV files are present, they take precedence over hardcoded defaults. Format:

- `results/emotion_accuracy.csv`: Columns: `emotion`, `precision`, `recall`, `f1`
- `results/retention_weeks.csv`: Columns: `week`, `retention_pct`
- `results/response_latency.csv`: Columns: `component`, `latency_ms`

## A.4 Database Schema

The backend uses Supabase PostgreSQL with the following key tables, organized by domain:

Table A.1: Core application tables.

| Table | Purpose | Key Columns |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `profiles` | User profiles | `id`, `display_name`, `level`, `xp_total`, `streak_count` |
| `chat_sessions` | Chat session metadata | `id`, `user_id`, `roadmap_id`, `created_at` |
| `chat_messages` | Conversation history | `id`, `session_id`, `role`, `content`, `metadata`, `created_at` |
| `assessments` | Clinical assessments | `id`, `user_id`, `assessment_type`, `score`, `severity`, `created_at` |
| `journal_entries` | User journaling | `id`, `user_id`, `title`, `content`, `mood_rating` |
| `badges` | Earned badges | `id`, `user_id`, `badge_id`, `earned_at` |
| `achievements` | Unlocked achievements | `id`, `user_id`, `achievement_id`, `progress`, `unlocked_at` |
| `challenges` | Active challenges | `id`, `user_id`, `challenge_id`, `progress`, `expires_at` |
| `crisis_reports` | Safety audit log | `id`, `user_id`, `trigger_text`, `action_taken`, `created_at` |
| `mood_logs` | Mood tracking | `id`, `user_id`, `mood_score`, `emotion_label`, `logged_at` |
| `sleep_logs` | Sleep tracking | `id`, `user_id`, `duration_minutes`, `quality`, `logged_at` |
| `roadmaps` | Personalized roadmaps | `id`, `user_id`, `overview`, `suggested_tasks`, `created_at` |

Table A.2: Community feature tables (13 tables).

| Table | Purpose | Key Columns |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `community_groups` | Support groups | `id`, `name`, `description`, `type`, `created_by` |
| `community_group_members` | Group membership | `id`, `group_id`, `user_id`, `role`, `joined_at` |
| `community_posts` | Group posts | `id`, `group_id`, `author_id`, `title`, `content`, `created_at` |
| `community_comments` | Post comments | `id`, `post_id`, `author_id`, `content`, `created_at` |
| `community_likes` | Post/comment likes | `id`, `target_id`, `user_id`, `target_type` |
| `community_reports` | Content reports | `id`, `reporter_id`, `target_id`, `reason`, `status` |
| `community_messages` | Group chat messages | `id`, `channel_id`, `author_id`, `content`, `created_at` |
| `community_channels` | Chat channels | `id`, `group_id`, `name`, `type`, `created_at` |
| `community_notifications` | User notifications | `id`, `user_id`, `type`, `payload`, `read`, `created_at` |
| `community_banned_users` | Group bans | `id`, `group_id`, `user_id`, `reason`, `banned_at` |
| `community_moderation_log` | Moderator actions | `id`, `moderator_id`, `action`, `target_id`, `created_at` |
| `community_user_profiles` | Community profiles | `id`, `user_id`, `display_name`, `avatar_url`, `bio` |
| `community_pinned_posts` | Pinned announcements | `id`, `post_id`, `group_id`, `pinned_by`, `pinned_at` |

Row-level security (RLS) policies ensure that each user can only access their own data in core tables, and only group members can read community messages.

## A.5 API Endpoints

The FastAPI backend exposes the following key endpoints, organized by service router:

Table A.3: Gateway endpoints (orchestration).

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose |
|--------|----------|---------|
| `POST` | `/api/assess` | Run GAD-7/PHQ-9 assessment chain (assessment → architect → auditor) |
| `POST` | `/api/roadmap` | Generate personalized 90-day roadmap |
| `GET` | `/health` | Health check (backend + knowledge base status) |

Table A.4: Domain service endpoints.

| Method | Endpoint | Service | Purpose |
|--------|----------|---------|---------|
| `POST` | `/api/chat/message` | Chat | Send message, receive AI response |
| `GET` | `/api/chat/history/{session_id}` | Chat | Retrieve conversation history |
| `POST` | `/assessment/assess` | Assessment | Direct assessment scoring |
| `GET` | `/knowledge_base/health` | Knowledge Base | ChromaDB health check |
| `POST` | `/ingestion/upload` | Ingestion | Upload and process clinical PDFs |
| `GET` | `/auditor/health` | Auditor | Auditor service health check |
| `GET` | `/api/telemetry/usage` | Telemetry | Usage analytics |
| `POST` | `/api/journal` | Journal | Create journal entry |
| `GET` | `/api/journal/entries` | Journal | List journal entries |
| `GET` | `/api/roadmap/{user_id}` | Roadmap | Retrieve roadmap for user |

Table A.5: Community endpoints (Supabase Edge Functions and Realtime).

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose |
|--------|----------|---------|
| `POST` | `/rest/v1/community_groups` | Create support group |
| `GET` | `/rest/v1/community_groups` | List groups |
| `POST` | `/rest/v1/community_group_members` | Join group |
| `DELETE` | `/rest/v1/community_group_members` | Leave group |
| `POST` | `/rest/v1/community_posts` | Create post |
| `GET` | `/rest/v1/community_posts` | List group posts |
| `POST` | `/rest/v1/community_comments` | Comment on post |
| `POST` | `/rest/v1/community_likes` | Like post/comment |
| `POST` | `/rest/v1/community_messages` | Send chat message |
| `GET` | `/rest/v1/community_messages` | Get chat history |
| `POST` | `/rest/v1/community_reports` | Report content |
| `POST` | `/rest/v1/community_banned_users` | Ban user from group |
| `POST` | `/rest/v1/community_notifications` | Send notification |
| `PATCH` | `/rest/v1/community_notifications` | Mark as read |
| `PATCH` | `/rest/v1/community_pinned_posts` | Pin post |

All gateway and domain endpoints require JWT authentication via Bearer token. The gateway enforces rate limiting (10 requests per minute on `/api/assess`, 30 requests per minute on `/api/chat`). Community endpoints use Supabase Row-Level Security policies rather than custom authentication middleware.

## A.6 Publication

No publications have resulted from this project at the time of submission. Future publications may target venues such as:

- ACM Conference on Human-Computer Interaction (CHI)
- Journal of Medical Internet Research (JMIR)
- IEEE Conference on Artificial Intelligence (CAI)
